content.problemStatement:
You are tasked with creating a simple calculator program in Python that can perform addition between two numbers. Create a class called Calculator with the following functionality:


Calculator Class:

Implement a constructor to initialize data.
add(Calculator other): Adds the values of the two Calculator objects and returns the result as a new Calculator object.


Main Function: Perform addition using the add method and display the result.
TaskWrite the methods of calculator class to display the addition.


Input Format
First line contain two integers for addition using add method.



Output Format
Print the result of addition on first line.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases (1 ≤ T ≤ 1000). Each of the next T lines contains two space‑separated integers a and b (|a|, |b| ≤ 10^9).
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the sum a + b.

sampleTestCases.explanation: There is one test case. Adding 1 and 2 yields 3.

solution.approach: Read the integer T. Loop T times, each time read two integers a and b, compute their sum using 64‑bit arithmetic, and output the result. The algorithm is O(T) time and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – one addition per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are stored.

code.Python3:
import sys

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out = []
    for _ in range(T):
        a = int(next(it))
        b = int(next(it))
        out.append(str(a + b))
    sys.stdout.write('
'.join(out))

if __name__ == '__main__':
    main()
